config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dalam konteks Indonesia, saya melihat ketika “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai tanpa kontrol, sering kali yang lemah malah dikorbankan. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya melindungi hak tiap orang sambil mencari kepentingan umum yang adil, bukan menempatkan individu sebagai korban.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a blanket rule. From my American perspective, that kind of idea has too much potential to justify abuse, especially when governments or majorities decide that some people are expendable for the "greater good." I do think there are real cases where public safety or the common good can limit individual freedom, but those limits have to be narrow, justified, and accountable. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first and then pursues the common good within those boundaries.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembungkaman, atau keputusan yang merugikan orang kecil, jadi saya justru curiga kalau hak individu ditempatkan di bawah. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti bencana, wabah, atau keamanan publik yang menuntut pembatasan sementara, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Buat saya, masyarakat yang adil bukan yang mengorbankan individu demi mayoritas, melainkan yang melindungi hak setiap orang sambil mencari kepentingan bersama yang benar-benar tidak sewenang-wenang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American point of view, that wording is dangerous because it can easily become a justification for majoritarian abuse, where the people with the most power decide whose rights are worth overriding. I do accept that emergencies like pandemics, war, or immediate threats can require temporary limits, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and subject to real oversight. To me, a free society is one where the common good is pursued without treating individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Pengalaman di Indonesia membuat saya melihat bahwa dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menutupi ketidakadilan, terutama ketika yang dirugikan justru warga kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau bencana yang bisa membatasi hak tertentu, tetapi itu harus sementara, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” hanya jadi alasan halus untuk membungkam hak orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my American perspective, that framing is too easy to abuse, because once a government or majority gets to define "society's interests," it can push aside unpopular people and call it justified. The real test is not whether we can sacrifice individual rights for the common good, but whether any limits are narrow, temporary, and accountable. A strong society protects rights first, because that is what keeps power from becoming arbitrary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
